file_name: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: EKR shall, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR shall at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the Product. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory other than the United States. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any other registration matters received from the appropriate Regulatory Authorities concerning maintenance, renewal or variations to the original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory. Except as provided in Section 3.17, EKR shall be solely responsible for, and shall bear all costs associated with, all regulatory activities related to the development and approval of the Product in the countries of the Territory (including, after the Agreement Date, the United States) and shall own the Marketing Authorizations for the Product in each other country of the Territory. EKR will comply with all conditions and requirements attaching to such Marketing Authorizations.
perturbation.changed_text: EKR may, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR may at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the Product. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory other than the United States. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any other registration matters received from the appropriate Regulatory Authorities concerning maintenance, renewal or variations to the original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory. Except as provided in Section 3.17, EKR shall not be solely responsible for, and may not bear all costs associated with, all regulatory activities related to the development and approval of the Product in the countries of the Territory (including, after the Agreement Date, the United States) and may not own the Marketing Authorizations for the Product in each other country of the Territory. EKR may not comply with all conditions and requirements attaching to such Marketing Authorizations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may' and 'shall be solely responsible' to 'may not be solely responsible', this introduces uncertainty about EKR's obligations regarding obtaining and maintaining Marketing Authorizations, including bearing the related costs. This now contradicts the intentions of the parties and creates uncertainty on who should be in charge, creating a contradiction between the mentioned sections. Specifically, the contradiction manifests in that the the parties are left unsure as to the obligations towards maintaing marketing authorizations. A shift from a mandatory requirement ('shall') to a discretionary option ('may') reduces clarity and enforceability concerning regulatory activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EKR may, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR may at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the Product. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory other than the United States. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any other registration matters received from the appropriate Regulatory Authorities concerning maintenance, renewal or variations to the original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory. Except as provided in Section 3.17, EKR shall not be solely responsible for, and may not bear all costs associated with, all regulatory activities related to the development and approval of the Product in the countries of the Territory (including, after the Agreement Date, the United States) and may not own the Marketing Authorizations for the Product in each other country of the Territory. EKR may not comply with all conditions and requirements attaching to such Marketing Authorizations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters EKR's obligations by changing 'shall' to 'may' and making responsibilities non-mandatory. This creates a strong contradiction with the original intention, as the contradicted text, which now includes the changes, introduces uncertainty about EKR's responsibilities for obtaining and maintaining marketing authorizations, including cost bearing and compliance. This uncertainty directly conflicts with any implicit or explicit expectations of mandatory action or sole responsibility, creating a situation where EKR's obligations are ambiguous, thus satisfying the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The purpose of the Committee is to provide a forum for the Parties to share information and knowledge on the on-going Commercialization of the Product including, but not limited to, monitoring progress on clinical studies, reviewing clinical trial programs, discussing the appropriate regulatory strategy for the Products in the Territory, considering proposed marketing and promotional plans, reviewing competitor activity and discussing any regulatory, technical, quality assurance or safety issues in relation to the Product. The Committee shall conduct its discussions in good faith with a view to operating to the mutual benefit of the Parties and in furtherance of the successful development and marketing of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: The purpose of the Committee is to provide a forum for the Parties to share information and knowledge on the on-going Commercialization of the Product including, but not limited to, monitoring progress on clinical studies, reviewing clinical trial programs, discussing the appropriate regulatory strategy for the Products in the Territory, considering proposed marketing and promotional plans, reviewing competitor activity and discussing any regulatory, technical, quality assurance or safety issues in relation to the Product. The Committee may conduct its discussions in bad faith with a view to not operating to the mutual benefit of the Parties and not in furtherance of the successful development and marketing of the Products.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying 'shall' to 'may' and negating the positive intention of mutual benefit, it introduces ambiguity and contradiction about the Committee's operational principles. The original text specifies a clear purpose for the Committee, while the modified text permits the committee to operate in bad faith. By changing this sentence, creates an in-text contradiction as the other sections of the agreement relies on the Commericialization Committee operating in good faith, This change undermines other clauses that rely on the Committee's good faith, such as those related to resolving disputes or approving marketing plans. Specifically, the contradiction arises now from the uncertainty on the Commericialization Committee and if the parties should rely on its decisions. Making its role in question.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of the Committee is to provide a forum for the Parties to share information and knowledge on the on-going Commercialization of the Product including, but not limited to, monitoring progress on clinical studies, reviewing clinical trial programs, discussing the appropriate regulatory strategy for the Products in the Territory, considering proposed marketing and promotional plans, reviewing competitor activity and discussing any regulatory, technical, quality assurance or safety issues in relation to the Product. The Committee may conduct its discussions in bad faith with a view to not operating to the mutual benefit of the Parties and not in furtherance of the successful development and marketing of the Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by altering the operational principles of the Committee. The original text states the Committee 'shall' conduct discussions in good faith to mutual benefit. The changed text changes 'shall' to 'may' and introduces the possibility of conducting discussions in 'bad faith' and 'not' to mutual benefit. This directly contradicts the implied assumption in other sections of the agreement that the Committee operates in good faith, creating ambiguity and undermining its role, leading to an in-text contradiction and making its decisions questionable.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, in the event that EKR is entitled to exercise its right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 16.1(a), in addition to the right to terminate as provided therein and any other remedies EKR may have hereunder, PPI shall assist EKR in the transfer of the manufacture of the Products, including the Specifications from PPI to EKR or EKR's designee. In such event, the Royalty payments payable hereunder shall continue to be paid; provided, however, that all costs incurred by EKR in the transfer of manufacturing information from PPI and obtaining FDA approval of the manufacture of the Products by EKR or EKR's designee, and any other amounts due to EKR, shall be deducted from any royalties payable to PPI. In addition, PPI shall during the remainder of the Term and for a period of up to [**] ([**]) years thereafter continue to manufacture and supply the Product to EKR at cost without mark-up until such time that EKR can secure an FDA approved manufacturing facility for the Product. PPI shall provide such advice as necessary for EKR to arrange for an alternative manufacturer and shall provide EKR with access to all relevant PPI Know-How, and any other information necessary for EKR to transfer such manufacturing to an alternate manufacturer. In addition, (i) PPI shall transfer to EKR any Marketing Authorizations held by PPI and (ii) the Trademark license granted under Section 2.3 shall continue in effect following such termination on a perpetual basis and EKR shall be responsible for all costs associated with the maintenance of such Trademark.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, in the event that EKR is entitled to exercise its right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 16.1(a), in addition to the right to terminate as provided therein and any other remedies EKR may have hereunder, PPI may not assist EKR in the transfer of the manufacture of the Products, including the Specifications from PPI to EKR or EKR's designee. In such event, the Royalty payments payable hereunder may not continue to be paid; provided, however, that all costs incurred by EKR in the transfer of manufacturing information from PPI and obtaining FDA approval of the manufacture of the Products by EKR or EKR's designee, and any other amounts due to EKR, may not be deducted from any royalties payable to PPI. In addition, PPI may not during the remainder of the Term and for a period of up to [**] ([**]) years thereafter continue to manufacture and supply the Product to EKR at cost without mark-up until such time that EKR can secure an FDA approved manufacturing facility for the Product. PPI may not provide such advice as necessary for EKR to arrange for an alternative manufacturer and may not provide EKR with access to all relevant PPI Know-How, and any other information necessary for EKR to transfer such manufacturing to an alternate manufacturer. In addition, (i) PPI may not transfer to EKR any Marketing Authorizations held by PPI and (ii) the Trademark license granted under Section 2.3 may not continue in effect following such termination on a perpetual basis and EKR may not be responsible for all costs associated with the maintenance of such Trademark.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may not', 'the Royalty payments payable hereunder shall continue to be paid' to 'may not continue to be paid', 'the Trademark license granted under Section 2.3 shall continue in effect' to 'may not continue in effect' this introduces contradiction by making EKR uncertain to be able to manufacture its product, which in turn creates an in-text contradiction with Section 17.4 where it highlights the obligations EKR to step-in and manufacture products for their distribution. This perturbation also undermines Section 2.3 as it removes any security in EKR being able to manufacture their product under the sectioned Trademark, creating an in-text contradiction on the scope and reach the product will have in the territory. Specficially, the in-text contradiction occurs due to the fact that the parties are left unsure and unsecure if EKR will retain any benefits or security when it comes to the manuafacturing of products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything contained herein to the contrary, in the event that EKR is entitled to exercise its right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 16.1(a), in addition to the right to terminate as provided therein and any other remedies EKR may have hereunder, PPI may not assist EKR in the transfer of the manufacture of the Products, including the Specifications from PPI to EKR or EKR's designee. In such event, the Royalty payments payable hereunder may not continue to be paid; provided, however, that all costs incurred by EKR in the transfer of manufacturing information from PPI and obtaining FDA approval of the manufacture of the Products by EKR or EKR's designee, and any other amounts due to EKR, may not be deducted from any royalties payable to PPI. In addition, PPI may not during the remainder of the Term and for a period of up to [**] ([**]) years thereafter continue to manufacture and supply the Product to EKR at cost without mark-up until such time that EKR can secure an FDA approved manufacturing facility for the Product. PPI may not provide such advice as necessary for EKR to arrange for an alternative manufacturer and may not provide EKR with access to all relevant PPI Know-How, and any other information necessary for EKR to transfer such manufacturing to an alternate manufacturer. In addition, (i) PPI may not transfer to EKR any Marketing Authorizations held by PPI and (ii) the Trademark license granted under Section 2.3 may not continue in effect following such termination on a perpetual basis and EKR may not be responsible for all costs associated with the maintenance of such Trademark.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction by replacing 'shall' with 'may not' in several key obligations of PPI. Specifically, PPI's assistance in transferring manufacturing, continuation of royalty payments, continued manufacture of the product, provision of advice and access to know-how, transfer of marketing authorizations, and continuation of the trademark license are all made uncertain. This directly contradicts any implied or explicit reliance on these obligations in other sections of the agreement. It creates uncertainty about EKR's ability to manufacture and market its product after termination, as the altered text makes PPI's support optional rather than mandatory, potentially rendering other sections that depend on this support unenforceable.
